title: NOC 75201 – Delivery service drivers and door-to-door distributors | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 75201 professionals are the indispensable "Final Link" in Canada’s multi-billion dollar e-commerce and logistics chain. As a TEER 3 occupation (reclassified from Skill Level C), these drivers and distributors operate light trucks or automobiles to deliver everything from retail parcels and medical supplies to hot food and promotional materials. In 2026, as Canada mandates the transition to Electric Delivery Vans for urban logistics, these workers are classified as Essential Service Talent. For immigration, this role is a primary target for the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway and the Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group75 – Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourersSub-Major Group752 – Other transport drivers, operators and related occupationsMinor Group7520 – Other transport drivers, operators and related occupationsNOC Code75201

duties: Load Management: Load and unload trucks or automobiles with parcels, products, or materials manually or using small equipment like hand trucks.
Navigation: Plan delivery routes using GPS and specialized dispatch software to ensure all items are delivered within strict timeframes.
Delivery: Drive vehicles to various locations to deliver items to residential or commercial addresses; verify addresses and obtain signatures where required.
Safety & Compliance: Inspect the vehicle for mechanical safety; ensure cargo is properly secured; adhere to all provincial traffic laws and municipal parking bylaws.
Inventory & Tech: Use handheld digital devices to record deliveries, update inventory levels, and process payments (COD) if necessary.
Distribution: (Distributors) Distribute flyers, newspapers, or promotional samples to households or businesses on a designated route.
Customer Service: Provide basic information to customers regarding delivery status and handle returns or damaged goods according to company policy.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Licensing (Mandatory): * A valid Class 5 Driver’s License (standard passenger vehicle) is the minimum requirement.A clean driving record (Driver’s Abstract) is essential.
A clean driving record (Driver’s Abstract) is essential.
Training: On-the-job training is provided, typically covering route software and safety protocols.
Physical Requirements: Physical stamina for frequent lifting (up to 50 lbs) and a fast-paced work environment is mandatory.
Many employers require a background check (Criminal Record Check) for home delivery roles.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced drivers for specialized sectors (e.g., Medical Couriers or Premium Vending) often earn annual salaries between $55,000 and $72,000 inclusive of overtime and performance bonuses.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that despite the development of autonomous delivery drones and sidewalk robots, the "human-in-the-loop" requirement for navigating complex apartment complexes and handling high-value items ensures strong job security. According to COPS, "Replacement Demand" due to the high turnover in the sector will create over 40,000 job openings across Canada through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for delivery service drivers in 2026 is Very Good to High, particularly in Canada’s urban corridors. In Ontario (GTA), British Columbia (Lower Mainland), and Alberta, a combination of permanent shifts in consumer behavior and a "Delivery-First" retail economy has created a significant labor deficit. According to the 2026 Logistics Labour Market report, delivery firms are seeing a 14% vacancy rate, making it an ideal time for experienced international drivers to secure LMIA-supported job offers.
The "EV Logistics" Frontier: Gain experience operating the newest fleets of electric delivery vehicles (EDVs) as companies like Amazon and Canada Post phase out combustion engines.
Rapid Workforce Entry: This is one of the fastest sectors for newcomers to enter, often requiring only a standard driver's license and minimal specialized training.
High-Tech Fieldwork: Utilize advanced route-optimization AI, handheld digital scanners, and real-time package tracking systems.
Strategic PR Pathway: This TEER 3 role is a priority for the Federal TR to PR programs and several provincial "In-Demand" streams.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A dedicated stream for 33,000 workers already in Canada, specifically targeting "Supply Chain and Delivery" roles.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): A high-priority stream for delivery drivers in the province's retail and logistics sectors.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): A direct route for drivers currently employed in British Columbia for at least 9 months.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for those with a job offer from a designated courier or delivery firm in an Atlantic port city.
